scope: Performance, Concurrency & Timing, Silent Failures in _pages/trading.html and _pages/live-trading.html

findings.title: Chart.js CDN failure silently breaks charts on trading.html
findings.claim: Charts should render and strategy navigation should work
findings.reality: No try/catch around Chart constructors. If Chart.js CDN is blocked, new Chart() throws ReferenceError. Charts render as empty boxes with no error message. Navigation functions survive due to hoisting but charts are dead.
findings.impact: Two empty chart areas with no user-visible error on corporate networks, ad-blocked environments, or CDN outages

findings.title: Chart.js CDN failure causes unhandled promise rejection on live-trading.html
findings.claim: Live trading dashboard should render portfolio data and equity curve chart
findings.reality: render() is async, called at line 525 with no .catch(). Chart.js failure at line 457 throws ReferenceError inside async function, producing unhandled promise rejection. Data tables render but chart is empty.
findings.impact: Partial page render with dead chart section and unhandled promise rejection in console

findings.title: Synchronous Chart.js load (201KB) blocks page rendering
findings.claim: Pages should load quickly for a personal portfolio site
findings.reality: Chart.js (205,889 bytes) loaded synchronously without async/defer. On live-trading.html, this blocks execution of render() which populates ALL page content, not just the chart.
findings.impact: On slow connections, live-trading.html shows empty skeleton for 1+ seconds while Chart.js downloads

findings.title: Google Fonts loaded as render-blocking resource in head
findings.reality: Google Fonts CSS link in <head> is render-blocking. 9 font weights (5 Inter + 2 Fira Code) requested. display=swap mitigates FOIT but CSS itself still blocks.
findings.impact: First Contentful Paint delayed on all pages by font CSS download time

findings.title: Unhandled promise rejection from render() with no user feedback
findings.claim: Code comments describe future remote data fetching from GitHub Gist or raw URL
findings.reality: render() has no try/catch, called with no .catch(). When switching from dummy data to live fetch, any network failure silently produces empty dashboard with no error message.
findings.impact: Future data source migration will produce silent empty dashboards on fetch failures

findings.title: Duplicate Chart.js load without prefetch optimization
findings.claim: Users may navigate between Trading and Live Trading pages
findings.reality: Both pages load Chart.js independently. No <link rel=prefetch> on other pages. First visit to either trading page requires full 201KB download. Browser cache helps on second visit.
findings.impact: First-time visitors to trading pages wait for full Chart.js download with no prefetch warming

findings.title: 36KB inline page with 9 hidden detail sections always in DOM
findings.claim: Trading page shows clickable strategy overview with detail views
findings.reality: All 9 strategy detail sections (294 divs, 99 trs, 326 tds) are always in DOM, hidden via CSS. 36.6KB page is 16x larger than next largest page.
findings.impact: Negligible on modern browsers. Standard pattern for small content sets.

findings.title: Unescaped ampersand in HTML table header
findings.claim: Positions table should display P&L column header
findings.reality: <th>P&L</th> uses unescaped ampersand. Should be P&L. Browsers display correctly but fails HTML validation.
findings.impact: No visible impact. Standards compliance issue only.

findings.title: Inline scripts and styles incompatible with strict CSP
findings.claim: Site hosted on GitHub Pages with standard security
findings.reality: Both pages use inline <style>, inline <script>, and 18 onclick handlers. Incompatible with strict Content Security Policy. GitHub Pages does not currently enforce CSP.
findings.impact: No current impact. Theoretical future breakage if CSP is enforced.
